last_item: Band-1.5: surface 'last good backup was N ago' prominently — closed the Backups-page gap (Dashboard + backend field already done/tested)
last_result: done — Dashboard already surfaced the latest backup (result/type/when/age) and the backend field store.LatestSuccessfulBackup was already covered by TestBackupHistory. The real gap was the Backups page: it buried freshness in the history table and gave NO loud signal when the most recent backup failed while an older one succeeded. Added exported pure helper backupFreshness(backups) classifying the newest-first history into none/good/stale/never-good, and a BackupStatusSummary banner rendered prominently under the page header — danger tone for none/stale/never-good, ok banner 'Last good backup N ago (type)' otherwise. Frontend-only (web/src/views/Backups.tsx). vitest/RTL test web/src/views/Backups.test.tsx (9 tests): helper classifications + rendered tone/title per state, with stale/fail cases pinned to the EXACT server result string 'fail' (internal/backup/manager.go:157 writes 'fail'/'success'). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no production bug; adopted its test-quality fixes (pin to real 'fail', reframe unknown-result case, add never-good render test). Full gate green (Go gofmt/vet/test/build + web typecheck/build/test). Note: dist rebuilt earlier in the iteration but unchanged by the test-only follow-up.
notes: Band 1.5 (data durability) IN PROGRESS. Done: (1) surface 'last good backup was N ago' (Dashboard card pre-existed; added Backups-page BackupStatusSummary banner + backupFreshness helper, exported from web/src/views/Backups.tsx for testing). Remaining 1.5: (2) loud ALERT when a scheduled backup fails or hasn't succeeded within its window — this is the natural next item; the freshness helper now encodes none/stale/never-good states the alert can mirror server-side. See internal/scheduler (has tests) + internal/store/alert.go + server handlers_alerts.go for the alert-rule pattern. (3) nudge backups off-host — S3 recommended default, warn on local-only (the Backups page already has an isLocal warn callout; check Settings copy + whether a default/recommendation is surfaced at provision). (4) restore-test surfacing (handleRestoreTest is currently a 'not implemented' stub returning InternalError). Backups plumbing: internal/backup (manager.go writes result 'fail'/'success', objectstore.go), internal/store/backup.go (LatestSuccessfulBackup, ListBackups newest-first). Web test conventions: vitest run, RTL/jsdom, `npm test` in web/; Callout/Badge are provider-free so summary components render without Router/Toast. Server result strings are free-form; the web helper matches 'success'/'fail' (canonical) plus defensive aliases. go/psql/pg_ctl are snap-sandboxed (run with sandbox disabled). TMPDIR empty — use /tmp.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
